---
title: "flextools Optimization — Full Benchmark"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    toc_depth: 3
    theme: flatly
    highlight: tango
    code_folding: hide
    df_print: paged
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo      = TRUE,
  message   = FALSE,
  warning   = FALSE,
  fig.width = 9,
  fig.height = 5,
  cache     = TRUE      # cache expensive chunks; delete cache/ to re-run
)

# Load package and helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
devtools::load_all("..")
source("benchmark_helpers.R")

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(scales)

# ── Benchmark parameters (adjust here to trade speed vs. resolution) ──────────
W_SEQ_FINE   <- seq(0, 1, by = 0.05)   # 21 points for tradeoff curves
W_SEQ_COARSE <- seq(0, 1, by = 0.1)    # 11 points for sweeps
LAMBDA_SEQ   <- c(0, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.5, 1)
EFF_SEQ      <- c(1.0, 0.99, 0.97, 0.95, 0.90, 0.85)

# Battery hardware parameters (shared throughout)
BCAP <- 50   # kWh
BC   <- 4    # kW
BD   <- 4    # kW
```

---

> **How to run:** open this file in RStudio, then click **Knit**.
> The first knit may take several minutes — subsequent knits are fast thanks to chunk caching.

---

# 0 · Data & baselines {.tabset}

```{r data-setup, cache=TRUE}
library(lubridate)

# ── Source datasets ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# energy_profiles: full year 2023, 15-min resolution (35 040 rows)
profiles_week <- flextools::energy_profiles |>
  filter(isoweek(datetime) == 18)   # one representative week (672 rows)

profiles_year <- flextools::energy_profiles   # full year (35 040 rows)

# ── Demand optimization datasets ───────────────────────────────────────────────
opt_demand_week <- demand_opt_data(profiles_week)   # for sweeps (fast)
opt_demand_year <- demand_opt_data(profiles_year)   # for timing section

# ── Battery optimization datasets ─────────────────────────────────────────────
opt_battery_week <- battery_opt_data(profiles_week)
opt_battery_year <- battery_opt_data(profiles_year)

# ── Baselines (no optimization) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
base_demand  <- demand_baseline(opt_demand_week)
base_battery <- battery_baseline(opt_battery_week)
```

## Demand dataset

```{r data-demand-plot, cache=FALSE, fig.height=3.5}
opt_demand_week |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-datetime, names_to = "variable") |>
  dplyr::filter(variable %in% c("flexible", "production",
                                 "price_imported", "price_exported")) |>
  ggplot(aes(datetime, value, colour = variable)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ variable, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
  labs(title = "Input data — demand optimization",
       x = NULL, y = NULL, colour = NULL) +
  theme_bench() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```

## Battery dataset

```{r data-battery-plot, cache=FALSE, fig.height=3.5}
opt_battery_week |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-datetime, names_to = "variable") |>
  ggplot(aes(datetime, value, colour = variable)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ variable, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
  labs(title = "Input data — battery optimization",
       x = NULL, y = NULL, colour = NULL) +
  theme_bench() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```

## Baseline KPIs

```{r baseline-table, cache=FALSE}
tibble::tibble(
  Metric          = c("Cost (€)", "Peak from grid (kW)", "Peak to grid (kW)"),
  `Demand (no opt)` = c(
    round(base_demand$cost, 2),
    round(base_demand$peak_from_grid, 2),
    round(base_demand$peak_to_grid, 2)
  ),
  `Battery (no battery)` = c(
    round(base_battery$cost, 2),
    round(base_battery$peak_from_grid, 2),
    round(base_battery$peak_to_grid, 2)
  )
) |> knitr::kable()
```

---

# 1 · Demand optimization

The `optimize_demand()` function shifts flexible load in time to meet one of
three goals:

| `opt_objective` | Solver | Description |
|-----------------|--------|-------------|
| `"grid"` / `w = 1` | QP | Minimise squared net power exchanged with the grid |
| `"cost"` / `w = 0` | MILP | Minimise energy cost (import/export prices) |
| `w ∈ (0, 1)` | MIQP | Weighted combination of both |

**Key parameters:**

* **`w`** — blending weight; `w = 1` is pure grid, `w = 0` is pure cost.
* **`lambda`** — penalty on load *changes* between slots.  Larger values produce
  smoother profiles but may sacrifice objective quality.

## 1.1 · Tradeoff curve: peak reduction vs. energy cost

```{r demand-sweep-w, cache=TRUE}
sweep_d_w <- sweep_demand(
  opt_demand_week,
  w_seq      = W_SEQ_FINE,
  lambda_seq = 0
)
```

```{r demand-tradeoff-plot, cache=FALSE, fig.height=4.5}
# Express both KPIs as % improvement relative to baseline (positive = better)
tradeoff_d <- sweep_d_w |>
  mutate(
    peak_reduction_pct = (base_demand$peak_from_grid - peak_from_grid) /
                          base_demand$peak_from_grid * 100,
    cost_savings_pct   = (base_demand$cost - cost) /
                          abs(base_demand$cost) * 100
  ) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    c(peak_reduction_pct, cost_savings_pct),
    names_to  = "metric",
    values_to = "pct_improvement"
  ) |>
  mutate(metric = recode(metric,
    peak_reduction_pct = "Peak-from-grid reduction (%)",
    cost_savings_pct   = "Energy cost savings (%)"
  ))

# Find crossover (where the two curves are closest to equal)
crossover_d <- sweep_d_w |>
  mutate(
    peak_red  = (base_demand$peak_from_grid - peak_from_grid) /
                 base_demand$peak_from_grid * 100,
    cost_sav  = (base_demand$cost - cost) / abs(base_demand$cost) * 100,
    diff_abs  = abs(peak_red - cost_sav)
  ) |>
  slice_min(diff_abs, n = 1)

ggplot(tradeoff_d, aes(w, pct_improvement, colour = metric)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey70") +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = crossover_d$w,
    linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey40"
  ) +
  annotate("text",
    x     = crossover_d$w + 0.03,
    y     = max(tradeoff_d$pct_improvement) * 0.85,
    label = paste0("Crossover\nw ≈ ", crossover_d$w),
    size  = 3.5, colour = "grey30", hjust = 0
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.1)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("#E07B54", "#4E8098")) +
  labs(
    title    = "Demand optimization: peak reduction vs. energy cost savings",
    subtitle = "Both metrics expressed as % improvement over the unoptimized baseline (λ = 0)",
    x        = "w  (0 = pure cost  →  1 = pure grid)",
    y        = "% improvement vs. baseline",
    colour   = NULL
  ) +
  theme_bench()
```

**Reading the chart:** moving `w` from 0 → 1 trades cost savings for peak
reduction.  The crossover marks the `w` where both benefits are equal on a
percentage basis.  To the right of the crossover, peak reduction outpaces the
cost penalty; to the left, cost savings dominate.

> **Note on the cost metric:** `evaluate_cost()` measures only grid energy
> costs (import price × kWh imported − export price × kWh exported).  The
> `"cost"` optimization objective *also* includes balancing-market revenues
> (`price_turn_up` / `price_turn_down`), so the grid-energy-cost savings shown
> here may *understate* the true economic benefit of `w = 0`.  Conversely,
> `w = 1` (grid) often shows good grid-cost savings as a side effect of aligning
> demand with local solar production.

## 1.2 · Effect of λ on the tradeoff

```{r demand-sweep-lambda, cache=TRUE}
sweep_d_lam <- sweep_demand(
  opt_demand_week,
  w_seq      = W_SEQ_COARSE,
  lambda_seq = LAMBDA_SEQ
)
```

```{r demand-lambda-plot, cache=FALSE, fig.height=5}
sweep_d_lam |>
  mutate(
    peak_reduction_pct = (base_demand$peak_from_grid - peak_from_grid) /
                          base_demand$peak_from_grid * 100,
    cost_savings_pct   = (base_demand$cost - cost) /
                          abs(base_demand$cost) * 100,
    lambda_lab = paste0("λ = ", lambda)
  ) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    c(peak_reduction_pct, cost_savings_pct),
    names_to  = "metric",
    values_to = "pct_improvement"
  ) |>
  mutate(metric = recode(metric,
    peak_reduction_pct = "Peak-from-grid reduction (%)",
    cost_savings_pct   = "Energy cost savings (%)"
  )) |>
  ggplot(aes(w, pct_improvement, colour = lambda_lab, group = lambda_lab)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey80") +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_point(size = 1.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ metric, ncol = 2) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2)) +
  scale_colour_viridis_d(option = "plasma", end = 0.9) +
  labs(
    title    = "Effect of λ on the demand optimization tradeoff",
    subtitle = "Each line is a different ramping-penalty value",
    x        = "w  (0 = cost  →  1 = grid)",
    y        = "% improvement vs. baseline",
    colour   = "Lambda (λ)"
  ) +
  theme_bench()
```

```{r demand-lambda-single-w, cache=FALSE, fig.height=4}
# Fix w = 0.5, show how each KPI evolves with lambda
sweep_d_lam |>
  filter(w == 0.5) |>
  mutate(
    peak_reduction_pct = (base_demand$peak_from_grid - peak_from_grid) /
                          base_demand$peak_from_grid * 100,
    cost_savings_pct   = (base_demand$cost - cost) /
                          abs(base_demand$cost) * 100
  ) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    c(peak_reduction_pct, cost_savings_pct),
    names_to  = "metric",
    values_to = "pct_improvement"
  ) |>
  mutate(metric = recode(metric,
    peak_reduction_pct = "Peak-from-grid reduction (%)",
    cost_savings_pct   = "Energy cost savings (%)"
  )) |>
  ggplot(aes(lambda, pct_improvement, colour = metric)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey70") +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  scale_x_log10(
    breaks = LAMBDA_SEQ[LAMBDA_SEQ > 0],
    labels = as.character(LAMBDA_SEQ[LAMBDA_SEQ > 0])
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("#E07B54", "#4E8098")) +
  labs(
    title    = "Effect of λ at fixed w = 0.5 (demand)",
    subtitle = "Log scale — note how large λ collapses both objectives toward zero improvement",
    x        = "λ (log scale)",
    y        = "% improvement vs. baseline",
    colour   = NULL
  ) +
  theme_bench()
```

> **λ guidance**
> `lambda` adds a quadratic penalty on *changes* in the flexible load between
> consecutive time slots — it acts as a smoothness regulariser.
>
> * **λ = 0** (default): no smoothness constraint; the solver is free to make
>   sharp load ramps.  This gives maximum objective quality but may produce
>   "spiky" profiles that are hard to implement in practice.
> * **λ ≈ 0.01 – 0.1**: a good first range to try.  Profiles become noticeably
>   smoother with only a small sacrifice in peak / cost performance.
> * **λ ≥ 0.5**: strong smoothing.  The flexible load barely moves from its
>   original shape — both peak reduction and cost savings shrink toward zero.
>
> Recommended starting point: **λ = 0.05** for balanced smoothness.

---

# 2 · Battery optimization — cycle count

Battery cycles matter for battery lifetime.  More cycles = faster degradation.
The `n_cycles` metric here is **total equivalent full cycles over the
7-day dataset**: `n_cycles = −Σ min(B, 0) × (15/60) / Bcap`, where `B` is
in kW and the factor `15/60` converts 15-minute slots to hours (kWh).

## 2.1 · Efficiency vs. cycle count

```{r battery-sweep-eff, cache=TRUE}
sweep_b_eff <- sweep_battery(
  opt_battery_week,
  w_seq      = c(0, 0.5, 1),
  lambda_seq = 0,
  eff_seq    = EFF_SEQ,
  Bcap = BCAP, Bc = BC, Bd = BD
)
```

```{r battery-cycles-eff-plot, cache=FALSE, fig.height=4.5}
sweep_b_eff |>
  mutate(objective = w_label(w)) |>
  ggplot(aes(eff, n_cycles, colour = objective, group = objective)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = EFF_SEQ,
    labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("#E07B54", "#9B59B6", "#4E8098")) +
  labs(
    title    = "Battery total cycles (7-day) vs. round-trip efficiency",
    subtitle = "charge_eff = discharge_eff; λ = 0",
    x        = "Round-trip efficiency",
    y        = "Equivalent full cycles (7-day total)",
    colour   = NULL
  ) +
  theme_bench()
```

**Key insight:** the `"cost"` objective (w = 0) naturally reduces cycles when
efficiency drops below 1, because the efficiency-aware MILP internalises
round-trip losses in the SOC constraints — cycling becomes less attractive
when losses are high.  The `"grid"` objective (w = 1) is blind to costs and
may continue cycling even when inefficient.

## 2.2 · Lambda vs. cycle count

```{r battery-sweep-lambda-cycles, cache=TRUE}
sweep_b_lam <- sweep_battery(
  opt_battery_week,
  w_seq      = c(0, 0.5, 1),
  lambda_seq = LAMBDA_SEQ,
  eff_seq    = c(1, 0.95),
  Bcap = BCAP, Bc = BC, Bd = BD
)
```

```{r battery-cycles-lambda-plot, cache=FALSE, fig.height=5}
sweep_b_lam |>
  filter(lambda > 0) |>
  mutate(
    objective  = w_label(w),
    eff_lab    = paste0("η = ", scales::percent(eff, accuracy = 1))
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(lambda, n_cycles, colour = objective, group = objective)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  facet_wrap(~ eff_lab, ncol = 2) +
  scale_x_log10(
    breaks = LAMBDA_SEQ[LAMBDA_SEQ > 0],
    labels = as.character(LAMBDA_SEQ[LAMBDA_SEQ > 0])
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("#E07B54", "#9B59B6", "#4E8098")) +
  labs(
    title    = "Battery total cycles (7-day) vs. λ",
    subtitle = "Higher λ penalises rapid charge/discharge swings → fewer cycles",
    x        = "λ (log scale)",
    y        = "Equivalent full cycles (7-day total)",
    colour   = NULL
  ) +
  theme_bench()
```

> **Important:** when `lambda > 0`, the battery solver switches from MILP to
> continuous OSQP — this is *faster* but relaxes the binary import/export
> exclusivity constraint.  The smoothing and the solver change happen together.

## 2.3 · w vs. cycle count

```{r battery-sweep-w-cycles, cache=TRUE}
sweep_b_w <- sweep_battery(
  opt_battery_week,
  w_seq      = W_SEQ_COARSE,
  lambda_seq = 0,
  eff_seq    = c(1, 0.95),
  Bcap = BCAP, Bc = BC, Bd = BD
)
```

```{r battery-cycles-w-plot, cache=FALSE, fig.height=4.5}
sweep_b_w |>
  mutate(eff_lab = paste0("η = ", scales::percent(eff, accuracy = 1))) |>
  ggplot(aes(w, n_cycles, colour = eff_lab, group = eff_lab)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.1)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("#4E8098", "#E07B54")) +
  labs(
    title    = "Battery total cycles (7-day) vs. w (combined objective weight)",
    subtitle = "λ = 0",
    x        = "w  (0 = cost  →  1 = grid)",
    y        = "Equivalent full cycles (7-day total)",
    colour   = "Efficiency"
  ) +
  theme_bench()
```

---

# 3 · Battery optimization — energy cost

## 3.1 · Efficiency vs. cost change

```{r battery-cost-eff-plot, cache=FALSE, fig.height=4.5}
# Reuse sweep_b_eff from Section 2.1
sweep_b_eff |>
  mutate(
    cost_savings_pct = (base_battery$cost - cost) /
                        abs(base_battery$cost) * 100,
    objective = w_label(w)
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(eff, cost_savings_pct, colour = objective, group = objective)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey70") +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = EFF_SEQ,
    labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("#E07B54", "#9B59B6", "#4E8098")) +
  labs(
    title    = "Energy cost savings vs. round-trip efficiency",
    subtitle = "Positive = cheaper than no-battery baseline; λ = 0",
    x        = "Round-trip efficiency",
    y        = "Cost savings vs. no-battery baseline (%)",
    colour   = NULL
  ) +
  theme_bench()
```

## 3.2 · Lambda vs. cost change

```{r battery-cost-lambda-plot, cache=FALSE, fig.height=5}
# Reuse sweep_b_lam from Section 2.2
sweep_b_lam |>
  filter(lambda > 0) |>
  mutate(
    cost_savings_pct = (base_battery$cost - cost) /
                        abs(base_battery$cost) * 100,
    objective = w_label(w),
    eff_lab   = paste0("η = ", scales::percent(eff, accuracy = 1))
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(lambda, cost_savings_pct, colour = objective, group = objective)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey70") +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  facet_wrap(~ eff_lab, ncol = 2) +
  scale_x_log10(
    breaks = LAMBDA_SEQ[LAMBDA_SEQ > 0],
    labels = as.character(LAMBDA_SEQ[LAMBDA_SEQ > 0])
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("#E07B54", "#9B59B6", "#4E8098")) +
  labs(
    title    = "Energy cost savings vs. λ",
    x        = "λ (log scale)",
    y        = "Cost savings vs. no-battery baseline (%)",
    colour   = NULL
  ) +
  theme_bench()
```

## 3.3 · w vs. cost change

```{r battery-cost-w-plot, cache=FALSE, fig.height=4.5}
# Reuse sweep_b_w from Section 2.3
sweep_b_w |>
  mutate(
    cost_savings_pct = (base_battery$cost - cost) /
                        abs(base_battery$cost) * 100,
    eff_lab = paste0("η = ", scales::percent(eff, accuracy = 1))
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(w, cost_savings_pct, colour = eff_lab, group = eff_lab)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey70") +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.1)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("#4E8098", "#E07B54")) +
  labs(
    title    = "Energy cost savings vs. w",
    subtitle = "λ = 0",
    x        = "w  (0 = cost  →  1 = grid)",
    y        = "Cost savings vs. no-battery baseline (%)",
    colour   = "Efficiency"
  ) +
  theme_bench()
```

---

# 4 · Computation time

> ⚠️ This section runs the full timing sweep including year-length datasets.
> It may take **5–20 minutes** to complete on first run; results are cached.

```{r timing-data, cache=TRUE}
# Build datasets of three different lengths from the same energy_profiles year ─
data_lengths <- list(
  "1 week  (672 rows)"    = profiles_week,
  "1 month (2 976 rows)"  = flextools::energy_profiles |>
                              filter(lubridate::month(datetime) == 1),
  "1 year  (35 040 rows)" = profiles_year
)

objectives <- c("grid", "cost", "0.1", "0.5")

# ── Demand timing ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
timing_demand <- purrr::map(names(data_lengths), function(len_lab) {
  purrr::map(c(0, 0.1), function(lam) {
    purrr::map(objectives, function(obj_chr) {
      obj <- if (obj_chr %in% c("grid", "cost")) obj_chr else as.numeric(obj_chr)
      d   <- demand_opt_data(data_lengths[[len_lab]])
      res <- bench_demand(d, opt_objective = obj, lambda = lam)
      tibble::tibble(
        optimizer = "demand",
        objective = obj_chr,
        lambda    = lam,
        n_rows    = len_lab,
        time_sec  = res$time_sec
      )
    }) |> purrr::list_rbind()
  }) |> purrr::list_rbind()
}) |> purrr::list_rbind()

# ── Battery timing ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
timing_battery <- purrr::map(names(data_lengths), function(len_lab) {
  purrr::map(c(0, 0.1), function(lam) {
    purrr::map(objectives, function(obj_chr) {
      obj <- if (obj_chr %in% c("grid", "cost")) obj_chr else as.numeric(obj_chr)
      d   <- battery_opt_data(data_lengths[[len_lab]])
      res <- bench_battery(d, opt_objective = obj, lambda = lam,
                           Bcap = BCAP, Bc = BC, Bd = BD)
      tibble::tibble(
        optimizer = "battery",
        objective = obj_chr,
        lambda    = lam,
        n_rows    = len_lab,
        time_sec  = res$time_sec
      )
    }) |> purrr::list_rbind()
  }) |> purrr::list_rbind()
}) |> purrr::list_rbind()

timing_all <- dplyr::bind_rows(timing_demand, timing_battery)
```

## 4.1 · Summary table

```{r timing-table, cache=FALSE}
timing_all |>
  mutate(time_sec = round(time_sec, 2)) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from  = n_rows,
    values_from = time_sec
  ) |>
  arrange(optimizer, lambda, objective) |>
  knitr::kable(
    col.names = c("Optimizer", "Objective", "λ",
                  "1 week", "1 month", "1 year"),
    caption   = "Computation time (seconds)",
    align     = "llrrrr"
  )
```

## 4.2 · Bar chart

```{r timing-bar, cache=FALSE, fig.height=5}
timing_all |>
  mutate(
    lambda_lab = paste0("λ = ", lambda),
    objective  = factor(objective, levels = c("grid", "0.1", "0.5", "cost"))
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(objective, time_sec, fill = n_rows)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  facet_grid(optimizer ~ lambda_lab, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Blues", direction = -1) +
  labs(
    title    = "Computation time by objective, λ, dataset length and optimizer",
    subtitle = "Battery 'cost' with λ = 0 uses MILP — expect much longer runtimes",
    x        = "Objective",
    y        = "Time (seconds)",
    fill     = "Dataset length"
  ) +
  theme_bench() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")
```

> **Notes on solver choice:**
> * **`"grid"`** and combined objectives use a **QP** (quadratic program) solved
>   by OSQP — fast and scales linearly with time-series length.
> * **`"cost"`** with `lambda = 0` uses a **MILP** (mixed-integer linear program,
>   via HiGHS) to enforce binary import/export exclusivity — significantly
>   slower, especially for long datasets.
> * Setting **`lambda > 0`** for the cost objective switches the solver to OSQP
>   (continuous relaxation), recovering fast solve times at the expense of exact
>   binary constraints.

---

# 5 · Additional analyses

## 5.1 · Load duration curves

A load duration curve sorts power values from highest to lowest.  The area
under the curve is proportional to total energy imported from the grid.

```{r ldc-demand, cache=TRUE}
ldc_demand_res <- list(
  baseline  = list(profile = opt_demand_week$flexible),
  grid      = bench_demand(opt_demand_week, "grid"),
  cost      = bench_demand(opt_demand_week, "cost"),
  combined  = bench_demand(opt_demand_week, 0.5)
)
```

```{r ldc-demand-plot, cache=FALSE, fig.height=4.5}
production <- opt_demand_week$production

ldc_df <- purrr::imap(ldc_demand_res, function(res, nm) {
  O   <- res$profile
  net <- O - production
  imp <- pmax(net, 0)
  tibble::tibble(
    scenario = nm,
    rank     = seq_along(imp) / length(imp),
    imported = sort(imp, decreasing = TRUE)
  )
}) |> purrr::list_rbind()

ggplot(ldc_df, aes(rank, imported, colour = scenario)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c(baseline = "grey60", grid = "#4E8098",
               cost = "#E07B54", combined = "#9B59B6"),
    labels = c(baseline = "No optimization", grid = "Grid (w=1)",
               cost = "Cost (w=0)", combined = "Combined (w=0.5)")
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Load duration curve — imported power from grid (demand opt.)",
    subtitle = "Area under curve = total imported energy",
    x        = "% of time intervals (sorted by power)",
    y        = "Imported power (kW)",
    colour   = NULL
  ) +
  theme_bench()
```

```{r ldc-battery, cache=TRUE}
ldc_battery_res <- list(
  baseline = list(profile = rep(0, nrow(opt_battery_week))),
  grid     = bench_battery(opt_battery_week, "grid",  Bcap=BCAP,Bc=BC,Bd=BD),
  cost     = bench_battery(opt_battery_week, "cost",  Bcap=BCAP,Bc=BC,Bd=BD),
  combined = bench_battery(opt_battery_week, 0.5,     Bcap=BCAP,Bc=BC,Bd=BD)
)
```

```{r ldc-battery-plot, cache=FALSE, fig.height=4.5}
static_batt <- opt_battery_week$static
prod_batt   <- opt_battery_week$production

ldc_batt_df <- purrr::imap(ldc_battery_res, function(res, nm) {
  net <- res$profile + static_batt - prod_batt
  imp <- pmax(net, 0)
  tibble::tibble(
    scenario = nm,
    rank     = seq_along(imp) / length(imp),
    imported = sort(imp, decreasing = TRUE)
  )
}) |> purrr::list_rbind()

ggplot(ldc_batt_df, aes(rank, imported, colour = scenario)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c(baseline = "grey60", grid = "#4E8098",
               cost = "#E07B54", combined = "#9B59B6"),
    labels = c(baseline = "No battery", grid = "Grid (w=1)",
               cost = "Cost (w=0)", combined = "Combined (w=0.5)")
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Load duration curve — imported power from grid (battery opt.)",
    x        = "% of time intervals (sorted by power)",
    y        = "Imported power (kW)",
    colour   = NULL
  ) +
  theme_bench()
```

## 5.2 · Battery State-of-Charge profiles

```{r soc-profiles, cache=TRUE}
soc_scenarios <- list(
  grid     = ldc_battery_res$grid$profile,
  cost     = ldc_battery_res$cost$profile,
  combined = ldc_battery_res$combined$profile
)

soc_df <- purrr::imap(soc_scenarios, function(B, nm) {
  # get_storage_level returns length(power) elements (SOC at start of each slot)
  soc <- get_storage_level(B, init = 0, time_resolution = 15)
  tibble::tibble(
    scenario = nm,
    datetime = opt_battery_week$datetime,
    soc_pct  = soc / BCAP * 100
  )
}) |> purrr::list_rbind()
```

```{r soc-plot, cache=FALSE, fig.height=4.5}
ggplot(soc_df, aes(datetime, soc_pct, colour = scenario)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c(grid = "#4E8098", cost = "#E07B54", combined = "#9B59B6"),
    labels = c(grid = "Grid (w=1)", cost = "Cost (w=0)", combined = "Combined (w=0.5)")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), labels = scales::percent_format(scale = 1)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Battery State-of-Charge profiles by objective",
    subtitle = paste0("Bcap = ", BCAP, " kWh  |  Bc = Bd = ", BC, " kW  |  λ = 0"),
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "State of Charge (%)",
    colour   = NULL
  ) +
  theme_bench()
```

**Grid objective** charges when surplus solar is available and discharges to
avoid importing.  **Cost objective** charges at low-price hours (off-peak
night) and discharges during expensive peak hours — a classic arbitrage
pattern.  **Combined** shows intermediate behaviour.

## 5.3 · Sensitivity heatmap — peak reduction

```{r demand-heatmap-sweep, cache=TRUE}
heatmap_seq_w   <- seq(0, 1, by = 0.1)
heatmap_seq_lam <- c(0, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.5, 1)

sweep_heatmap <- sweep_demand(
  opt_demand_week,
  w_seq      = heatmap_seq_w,
  lambda_seq = heatmap_seq_lam
)
```

```{r demand-heatmap-plot, cache=FALSE, fig.height=5}
sweep_heatmap |>
  mutate(
    peak_reduction_pct = (base_demand$peak_from_grid - peak_from_grid) /
                          base_demand$peak_from_grid * 100,
    lambda_lab = paste0("λ=", lambda)
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(factor(w), factor(lambda), fill = peak_reduction_pct)) +
  geom_tile(colour = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", peak_reduction_pct)),
            size = 3, colour = "white") +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "magma", direction = -1, name = "Peak\nreduction (%)") +
  labs(
    title    = "Sensitivity heatmap: peak-from-grid reduction (demand opt.)",
    subtitle = "Each cell shows % peak reduction vs. unoptimized baseline",
    x        = "w  (0 = cost  →  1 = grid)",
    y        = "λ (ramping penalty)"
  ) +
  theme_bench() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")
```

## 5.4 · Round-trip efficiency: cost per kWh cycled

This analysis quantifies how much each kWh cycled through the battery costs
(or saves) — a direct measure of how efficiency losses erode economic
benefits.

```{r battery-cost-per-cycle, cache=TRUE}
sweep_b_eff_cost <- sweep_battery(
  opt_battery_week,
  w_seq      = c(0, 0.5, 1),
  lambda_seq = 0,
  eff_seq    = EFF_SEQ,
  Bcap = BCAP, Bc = BC, Bd = BD
)
```

```{r battery-cost-per-cycle-plot, cache=FALSE, fig.height=4.5}
sweep_b_eff_cost |>
  mutate(
    # n_cycles = total equiv. full cycles over 7 days (kWh discharged / Bcap)
    # total_kwh_cycled = n_cycles * Bcap gives total kWh discharged
    total_kwh_cycled = n_cycles * BCAP,
    extra_cost       = cost - base_battery$cost,
    # negative = savings per kWh; positive = extra cost per kWh
    eur_per_kwh = ifelse(
      total_kwh_cycled > 1e-6,
      extra_cost / total_kwh_cycled,
      NA_real_
    ),
    objective = w_label(w)
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(eur_per_kwh)) |>
  ggplot(aes(eff, eur_per_kwh, colour = objective, group = objective)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey70") +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = EFF_SEQ,
    labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("#E07B54", "#9B59B6", "#4E8098")) +
  labs(
    title    = "Net cost per kWh cycled vs. round-trip efficiency",
    subtitle = "Negative = savings; positive = extra cost compared to no-battery baseline",
    x        = "Round-trip efficiency",
    y        = "€ per kWh cycled",
    colour   = NULL
  ) +
  theme_bench()
```

**Interpretation:** at η = 1 the cost objective extracts maximum savings per
kWh cycled (buy cheap, sell expensive).  As efficiency drops, round-trip
losses erode those savings.  The grid objective may show increasing costs
per kWh cycled because it cycles aggressively regardless of prices.

---

# 6 · Summary

```{r summary-table, cache=FALSE}
tibble::tibble(
  Parameter = c(
    "w (combined weight)",
    "w (combined weight)",
    "λ (ramping penalty)",
    "λ (ramping penalty)",
    "λ (ramping penalty)",
    "Efficiency (battery)",
    "Efficiency (battery)",
    "Solver (demand)",
    "Solver (battery cost, λ=0)",
    "Solver (battery cost, λ>0)"
  ),
  Value = c(
    "0 (cost)", "1 (grid)",
    "0", "0.01–0.1", "≥ 0.5",
    "< 1", "= 1",
    "QP / MIQP (OSQP / HiGHS)",
    "MILP (HiGHS)", "QP (OSQP)"
  ),
  Effect = c(
    "Minimise energy cost; may increase peak",
    "Minimise peak; may increase cost",
    "No smoothing; sharpest profile changes",
    "Recommended range: smooth profiles, minor objective loss",
    "Heavy smoothing; profile barely moves, objective near baseline",
    "Fewer cycles; MILP internalises losses in SOC",
    "Efficiency ignored in constraints",
    "Fast, scales well with time-series length",
    "Slow (especially for long datasets)",
    "Fast (binary constraint relaxed)"
  )
) |>
  knitr::kable(caption = "Quick reference: parameter effects")
```

---

*Generated with flextools `r packageVersion("flextools")` on `r Sys.Date()`.*
